Title: Heterologous microProtein expression identifies LITTLE NINJA, a dominant regulator of jasmonic acid signaling
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Brachypodium distachyon; Hordeum vulgare; Oryza sativa
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: microProtein; dominant regulator of jasmonic acid signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NINJA-related microProtein containing NINJA Domain C
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: jasmonic acid signaling and hormone-response gene regulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: active jasmonate signaling compound controlling defense and developmental responses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ectopic expression alters jasmonic acid signaling, decreases JA-Ile levels in Brachypodium, reduces plant height, and increases tillering/branching
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; overexpression in Brachypodium, barley, and rice; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; yeast two-hybrid; yeast three-hybrid; MeJA treatment response assay; phytohormone profiling by UHPLC/TQ-MS
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interferes with NINJA repressor complexes; weakens NINJA homodimerization; proposed to affect JAZ/NINJA/TPL complex
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: JA-responsive genes including ERF1, ERF1A, ERF11 in Arabidopsis; JMT and other MeJA-responsive genes in Brachypodium
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes ectopic expression of a subset of JA-induced genes and repression of a subset of JA-downregulated genes, mimicking MeJA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein-protein interaction-mediated modulation of repressor complex activity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent dominant regulator/modulator of JA signaling that accounts for about 25% of JA-induced transcriptional changes in Brachypodium
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely negative feedback on JA biosynthesis/signaling, inferred from ectopic JA signaling with reduced JA-Ile levels
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected from Brachypodium immature spikelet cDNA; transgene expression analyzed in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expression detected in leaves/rosette leaves of transgenic plants
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: double CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in Arabidopsis; overexpression vector pIPKb003 for grasses, promoter not specified in provided text
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 µM methyl jasmonate spray treatment; mock treatment; long-day or short-day growth conditions depending on species
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced growth/stature; altered cytokinin and auxin-related hormone balance; no clear increased pathogen resistance in barley and no obvious enhanced resistance reported compared with jazD
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target to alter crop architecture and increase tillering/branching across cereals; can be created or enhanced by genome editing and promoter engineering
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE124493; GSE155487; phytohormone profiling by UHPLC/TQ-MS
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LNJ is a NINJA-related microProtein identified from Brachypodium that acts as a dominant regulator of jasmonic acid signaling. 2. Ectopic LNJ expression in Arabidopsis causes a stunted, jazD-like phenotype and induces overlapping JA-responsive transcriptome changes. 3. BdLNJ interacts with NINJA and weakens NINJA homodimerization, supporting a mechanism via interference with the JAZ/NINJA/TPL repression module. 4. Overexpression in Brachypodium reduces height, increases tillering, alters MeJA responsiveness, and decreases JA-Ile levels. 5. The architectural effect is transferable to barley and rice, supporting application in cereal engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LNJ was initially thought monocot-specific but later recognized as a splice isoform-related microProtein not strictly monocot-specific; likely produced by alternative splicing from a larger NINJA-like precursor in Brachypodium and maize.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: NINJA-domain regulatory protein; engineered source of LITTLE NINJA-like microProtein
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ABI5-BINDING PROTEIN2; contains EAR and NINJA domains
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: jasmonic acid signaling when converted into LNJ-like truncated isoform
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: active jasmonate signaling compound controlling defense and developmental responses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRISPR-generated truncated AFP2 isoform (afp2-cr6) causes reduced rosette size and jazD-like phenotype, indicating engineered LNJ-like proteins can alter JA-related growth outputs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9-mediated deletion engineering; RT-PCR isoform analysis; phenotyping of afp2-cr6 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Engineered deletion removes EAR and NINJA domains to create LITTLE NINJA-like isoform
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein truncation/genome editing to create dominant microProtein-like regulator
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Demonstrates that endogenous NINJA-domain proteins can be edited into microProtein-like JA signaling regulators
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Proof of concept for engineering LITTLE NINJA-like regulators directly from endogenous loci to modify crop architecture
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AFP2 encodes a larger NINJA-domain protein related to LNJ. 2. CRISPR deletions in AFP2 generated multiple altered protein isoforms, including afp2-cr6 lacking the N-terminal EAR and NINJA domains. 3. afp2-cr6 plants show reduced rosette size resembling jazD, demonstrating that LITTLE NINJA-like regulators can be engineered from endogenous genes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper studies AFP2 mainly as the Arabidopsis ancestral/engineered counterpart used to create an LNJ-like protein, not as a native full-length JA regulator under normal conditions.
